cards.title: CODE CHECK
cards.what: Compared the current code to the last saved version.
cards.why: Helps catch mistakes before they're permanent.
cards.next: Will probably save these changes next.

cards.title: SAVING PROGRESS
cards.what: Saved a checkpoint — like hitting 'Save' but it remembers every version.
cards.why: If anything breaks later, we can go back to this exact point.
cards.next: The code is now safely saved.

cards.title: QUALITY CHECK
cards.what: Running automated checks to make sure the code works correctly.
cards.why: Like a spell-checker but for logic — catches bugs before users do.

cards.title: INSTALLING TOOLS
cards.what: Downloading helper code that other people already wrote.
cards.why: Saves time — no need to reinvent the wheel.

cards.title: BUILDING
cards.what: Converting human-readable code into something the computer can run.
cards.why: Like compiling a recipe into an actual meal.
cards.next: If it succeeds, the code is ready to run.

cards.title: SHARING CODE
cards.what: Sending the saved code to a shared server so the team can see it.
cards.why: Like uploading a document to a shared drive.
cards.next: The team can now review these changes.

cards.title: SWITCHING VERSIONS
cards.what: Jumping to a different version of the code.
cards.why: Like opening a different draft of a document.
cards.next: Now working on a different set of changes.

cards.title: PROGRESS CHECK
cards.what: Asking 'what files have I changed so far?'
cards.why: A quick inventory before deciding what to do next.
cards.next: Usually followed by saving or reviewing changes.

cards.title: TALKING TO A SERVER
cards.what: Sending a request to another computer over the internet.
cards.why: Checking if an online service is working, or getting data.

cards.title: REMOVING FILES
cards.what: Deleting files that are no longer needed.
cards.why: Cleaning up — like throwing away rough drafts.
cards.next: These files are gone, but saved versions still exist.

fallback.title: WORKING
fallback.what: The main session ran a command.
